rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HANS HERBERT BARTSCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Requiring An Undertaking As A Condition For Issuance Of A Stay Of Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Ardnt Peltner's Motion for Order Requiring an Undertaking as a Condition for Issuance of Stay is DENIED. Peltner has to failed to meet his burden to show that an undertaking - particularly in the amount requested - is necessary or appropriate under the circumstances. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must so notify the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and other parties no later than 4 p.m. on Wednesday, October 24, 2018.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HANS HERBERT BARTSCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Norman Bartsch Herterichs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner Norman Herterich's Motion to Compel Discovery and Request for Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The Motion is granted with respect to Herterich's Request for Production ("RPD"), Set Five, Nos. 1 and 2. Alice Traeg was not Arndt Peltner's attorney in 2007 and thus the emails at issue are not attorney-client privileged. The Motion is denied with respect to Herterich's RPD, Set Six, No. 1. Work product privilege has already been asserted with respect to the documents sought, and Herterich's arguments that (1) Traeg waived work product protection of those documents when she produced them to Peltner and/or Tagliarini for the purposes of her deposition or (2) that work product protection somehow vanished when she was disbarred are unavailing and unsupported by authority. The notations in the privilege log reveal that the documents sought are Traeg's "notes to file," which almost certainly reflect her "impressions, conclusions, opinions, or legal research or theories," and are thus "not discoverable under any circumstances." (CCP § 2018.030(a).) Additionally, Herterich offers nothing demonstrating that he will be "unfairly prejudiced" in preparing his claim or defense or that it "will result in an injustice" if he does not obtain Traeg's notes. (CCP § 2018.030(b).) He has thus failed to meet his burden as the party moving to compel disclosure of the protected information. With respect to RPD, Set Six, No. 2, it is unclear what document(s) Herterich seeks in response to that RPD as there are no "undated" documents, "authored by Traeg and received by Peltner" that are identified as being subject to "attorney-product privilege." (See Exh. B to Briggs Decl. in Support of Motion, p. 3:10.) The Request for Sanctions is DENIED. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must so notify the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and other parties no later than 4 p.m. on Wednesday, October 24, 2018.
